questions.question: What does each node in a linked list contain?
choices: Data and a reference to the next node
Only data
Only a reference to the next node
A reference to the previous node only

questions.question: How does a linked list indicate the end of the sequence?
choices: The last node points to null
All nodes have no pointers
By setting a special end node
With a separate end marker variable

questions.question: What is an advantage of linked lists over arrays?
choices: Efficient insertions and deletions without shifting elements
Faster random access to elements
Elements stored in contiguous memory
Each node stores multiple data values

questions.question: Which type of linked list has nodes that reference both the next and the previous nodes?
choices: Doubly linked list
Singly linked list
Circular linked list
Single-ended list

questions.question: What is a drawback of linked lists compared to arrays?
choices: Random access is slower because traversal from the head is required
Insertion at the beginning is inefficient
Memory usage is always higher
Elements must be stored contiguously
